## Scope & Principles Followed (AGENTS.md + user spec + PHASE4 + PYTHON_ENTRYPOINTS + REMAINING... + REVIEW_CHECKLIST)
- Dogfooding: used agentforge-runner for task create (the thing replacing the py), worktree isolation, pre-commit "install" (hook verified active via common git dir), traceability on commit + task creation, self-check.
- Pre-audit mandatory with the bin/phase4 script.
- Only rm files with 0 active runtime callers (used grep -r --include="*.sh" --include="*.service" --include="*.py" limited + contexts to filter strings vs exec; post-surgical edits, 0 remain for the deleted).
- Tier1 + entrypoints from the two docs (22 files): full git rm after clean.
- Tier2 glue: surgical edits only (removed execs of deleted from sh, updated comments/inventory/echos in kept rollback tools (make_pure, disable_pure, enable_*, make_antigravity, trigger, execute, test_pure, install_services, consume), learning/* comments + __init__.py reexports, docs).
- Did NOT delete: core workers, eval non-flywheel, learning/utils+trajectory_dataset (core), safety/planning/long/obs (dual), mcp, watchdog, memory/rag, phase2_3_integration (surgical only if needed), post_process, parity dir, Tier3 (skill_improver etc), rust_flywheel_step.py, run_continuous etc.
- Isolation=worktree, branch agent/ per canonical.
- After: cargo check -p agentforge-runner (green), python -c tests (module path note but no import breakage of deleted), docs updated "deleted in Jules wave 2026-06-13".
- Trace every: this doc, commit msg refs task f29c675b, queue tasks created, AGENTS pre-commit/agent-review.
- At end: this record + self-check, prepare agent-review (skill not in env, so manual package via this + patch; would invoke `agent-review` or `/agent-review --to-jules` normally).
